On Tue, 21 Jan 2025 at 17:50, Peter Mandelson 
 wrote:
Thank you. I have thought about this. I have a large number of personal 
acquaintances with foreign nationals but I would not describe these as current 
friendships or personal business connections. I have no family members who 
are not listed previously.
Very best
Peter
From: 
Date: Thursday, 16 January 2025 at 09:42
To: Peter Mandelson 
Cc: 
Subject: Re: 20250115 - 
 - Additional Document Request - 
OSP
Good morning,
Thank you for enquiring about specifics of the foreign contacts form. 
UKSV appreciates that under the circumstances of your previous roles, 
you will have met and had contact with many foreign nationals over the 
years. Therefore for ease, please focus on the people who you have 
personal friendships with; have personal business connections to; family 
members that are not included elsewhere on the DV questionnaire. We 
will be looking to focus on those individuals that you have CURRENT or 
recent contact with.
Kind regards,
EDV Case Assistant
UKSV, Government Security Group
Cabinet Office
E: 
NSV Privacy Notice
On Wed, 15 Jan 2025 at 14:45, Peter Mandelson 
 wrote:
Do you mean literally every foreign national I have ever met ? I assume 
not. Could you be a bit more specific about what sort of person, how far 
back, who I have done more than meet ? Do you mean friends ? 
JCS
JCS
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Third Party
Personal
78


Counterpart ministers ? People in business ?
Thanks
Peter
Sent from my iPhone
On 15 Jan 2025, at 11:48, 
 
 wrote:
Handling Instruction: Vetting-in-Confidence
Good morning,
In addition to the documents requested, please complete the 
attached form as fully as possible, concentrating on current 
or recent contacts that you have. If this can be started ahead 
of the interview, the Vetting Clearance Office can use this to 
steer questioning.
Kind regards,
EDV Case Assistant
UKSV, Government Security Group
Cabinet Office
E: 
NSV Privacy Notice
Disclaimer. This email and any attachments to it may be confidential and are intended solely for the use of the 
individual to whom it is addressed. Any views or opinions expressed are solely those of the author and do not 
necessarily represent those of Global Counsel. If you are not the intended recipient of this email, you must 
neither take any action based upon its contents, nor copy or show it to anyone. Please contact the sender if you 
believe you have received this email in error. This email and its content are copyright © of Global Counsel and 
protected under UK and international law. All rights reserved. For the definition of Global Counsel please visit: 
https://www.global-counsel.com/disclaimer
JCS

parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
